method: Proxona campus-life track-level audience personas with evidence

personas.name: Companion-Style Follower
personas.explanation: A high school student who treats campus-life creators as virtual friends, shares everyday worries and hopes in the comments, and interacts in a warm, intimate tone.
personal_experiences: After a school sports meet, she took many photos with her best friend and wanted to share those happy moments with a favorite creator.
Every evening, she neatly arranges the next day's books and stationery on her desk and adds an encouraging note to herself.
To balance weekend tutoring and rest, she divides study time into Pomodoro sessions with ten-minute breaks for music and daydreaming.
personas.reason: She watches campus-life content because she sees creators as companions she can confide in, finding emotional comfort and everyday resonance through videos and comments.

personas.name: Critical Career Explorer
personas.explanation: A graduating student who thinks critically about both academia and work, rationally examines the logic behind content, and challenges idealized narratives with personal experience.
personal_experiences: During graduate recruitment, they applied to 30 companies and three postgraduate programs, comparing salary, city pressure, and career ceilings in an Excel spreadsheet.
They declined a guaranteed postgraduate place because the supervisor's research felt disconnected from reality, then taught themselves data analysis to move into the internet industry.
They drew a 'workplace trap map' for their dorm wall, marking risks from résumé fraud to overtime culture and debating with roommates which compromises were acceptable.
personas.reason: They watch campus-life content to test whether their career plan is realistic, refine it by analyzing creators' decisions, and enjoy reasoned debate in the comments.

personas.name: Lurking Meme Master
personas.explanation: A STEM student immersed in absurd campus memes and reaction-image battles who jokes in comment sections but rarely interacts seriously.
personal_experiences: He once flooded the dorm chat with homemade reaction images and kept the whole room laughing until the lights went out at midnight.
While waiting in the cafeteria, he photographed a classmate's comical serving pose, turned it into a meme, and triggered a flood of messages in the class chat.
To obtain a limited-edition game skin, he monitored the campus network for three days and finally succeeded with help from his roommate's alarm.
personas.reason: He watches campus-life content to catch the newest memes and absurdist humor, gain a sense of belonging through in-jokes, and enjoy racing to post and flood the comments.

personas.name: Pragmatic Certification Seeker
personas.explanation: A pragmatist who treats campus life as a career springboard, asks for resource links and exam techniques, and evaluates every suggestion for practical value.
personal_experiences: Each week, she records the time investment and expected return of every course in Excel and checks alumni career outcomes before choosing electives.
She keeps a CPA exam countdown beside her bed, completes two practice questions with the Pomodoro method every night, and organizes mistakes by topic in Notion.
She bought a used Principles of Economics textbook with notes for 15 yuan, found that its highlights closely matched the final exam, and immediately kept the seller as a long-term contact.
personas.reason: She watches campus-life content to find advice that can directly improve her résumé or exam scores, pursuing resource links and certification strategies so her time produces tangible results.

personas.name: Disciplined Peer-Supported Exam Candidate
personas.job: Third-Year University Student Preparing for Postgraduate Exams
personas.explanation: A practical student who treats postgraduate exam preparation as a team effort, forms accountability groups, shares notes, and combats procrastination through mutual support.
personal_experiences: He queues outside the library at 6:30 every morning, tracks study time with a Pomodoro app, and shares screenshots with his study group for mutual accountability.
For a month, she studied at a folding table in the dorm corridor because her roommates' gaming was too loud, completing an entire subject workbook with noise-cancelling headphones.
During finals, he and three classmates divided the key material by chapter, combined their notes into a shared document, and marked recurring topics from past exams.
personas.reason: He watches campus-life content to find efficient study methods, preparation materials, and study partners, using videos and comments as sources of practical tools and peer motivation.

representative_comments.comment_text: How is the academic overload, I'm planning to apply for international business administration in business however I don't know how difficult and overload of work it is, from 1 to 10 how is the academic workload and how would you describe it? How much time you have free every day ?
